title: Building a Modern Portfolio with Next.js 14
excerpt: Learn how to build a production-ready developer portfolio using the latest Next.js features including App Router, Server Components, and more.
content:
# Building a Modern Portfolio with Next.js 14

In this article, we'll explore how to build a stunning developer portfolio using Next.js 14 with the App Router.

## Why Next.js?

Next.js offers the perfect balance of performance and developer experience. With server components, you get lightning-fast pages without sacrificing interactivity.

## Project Structure

A well-organized portfolio needs clear separation of concerns:

- **app/** — Your page routes
- **components/** — Reusable UI components
- **lib/** — Utilities and database access
- **public/** — Static assets

## Authentication

For a portfolio with a CMS dashboard, JWT-based authentication works perfectly. We use `jose` for edge-compatible token signing.

## Conclusion

With Next.js 14, you can build a world-class portfolio that's fast, SEO-friendly, and easy to manage.

title: Mastering TypeScript: Advanced Patterns
excerpt: Deep dive into advanced TypeScript patterns including conditional types, mapped types, template literals, and more to level up your code.

title: The Art of Clean Code: Principles That Matter
excerpt: Explore timeless clean code principles that every developer should know — from naming conventions to SOLID principles and beyond.
content:
# The Art of Clean Code

Writing code is easy. Writing **clean** code is an art form.

## Meaningful Names

Names should reveal intent. `d` tells us nothing. `elapsedTimeInDays` tells us everything.

## Single Responsibility

Every function should do one thing, and do it well. If you need "and" to describe what a function does, it does too much.

## Comments Are Lies

Code should be self-explanatory. Comments rot. Code doesn't lie (well, usually).
